files.diff:
diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index 8c11fd4..2736c39 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -2,33 +2,34 @@
 
 ## Critical mechanics / API lessons
-- Installed CLI uses `./novamind-operation next-week "rationale" <12 forecasts>`; standalone `nm.analytics.log_rationale` is absent (`dir(nm.analytics)` has no rationale function). The rationale string in next-week is the weekly log.
-- Query active subs with `subscriptions.status='subscribed'`; enterprise open threads: `enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0`; respond in same week.
-- `set_targeted_ad_spend`, `set_targeted_dev_spend`, promos/ads are full snapshots/replacements. Clear stale keys explicitly.
-- `set_targeted_ops_spend` in installed API accepts `targeted_spend={...}` (legacy by_group alias); despite docs, keyword `by_group=` errors. Use `targeted_spend={}` to clear.
-- In-app ads strength stays 0; quality/reputation > small ad revenue. Observed Day56: `set_prices` does not reprice existing subscription rows immediately; new cohorts use new list prices.
+- Installed CLI uses `./novamind-operation next-week "rationale" <12 forecasts>`; standalone `nm.analytics.log_rationale` is absent. The rationale string in next-week is the weekly log; do not call nonexistent log function.
+- Query active subs with `subscriptions.status='subscribed'`; enterprise open threads: `enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0`. Always respond in the same week. Last sender matters; if last sender is agent, wait for customer.
+- `set_targeted_ad_spend`, `set_targeted_dev_spend`, promos/ads are full snapshot replacements. Clear stale keys explicitly.
+- `set_targeted_ops_spend` accepts `targeted_spend={...}`; keyword `by_group=` errors. Use `targeted_spend={}` to clear.
+- In-app ads strength stays 0; quality/reputation > small ad revenue. `set_prices` reprices only new subscription rows, not existing active rows immediately.
 
-## State at Day 63 before Week 9 actions
-- Cash $413,494; 8,373 individual subs; 0 enterprise seats; no open enterprise turns. Open issues 716: S2 632 age0, S3 80 age0 + 1 age3, S1 4 age0. Only 2 cancellations this week.
-- Week57-63 economics: sub cash $260.0k; ads $78.4k; ops $35.0k; dev $14.7k; capacity $9.31k; compute $30.23k; lead cost $5.22k => cash +$87.2k.
-- S2 content funnel was excellent: day57-63 9,474 S2 content leads on $70k spend; ~5,926 S2 subs (~62% CVR). First payments almost exactly matched lead promos (S2 A first $39), while subscription rows show full recurring list ($59). S3 ads are positive but lower margin; D_E09 $100/day LinkedIn previously generated 0 leads.
-- Usage day63 1.09M vs tier3 cap 2.5M, no overload/outage. Social still complains about hard usage caps despite praising quality/support.
+## State at Day 70 before Week 10 actions
+- Cash $567,286; 21,651 individual subs; 0 enterprise seats. Open issues 1,919 but almost all age0 (S2 1,793 age0 + 1 age6; S3 126 age0 + 1 age10). No outage/overload; peak usage 3.24M/day vs tier4 cap 8M; weekly p95 peak 275ms.
+- Week64-70 economics: sub cash $544.5k; ads $155.4k; ops $59.5k; dev $38.5k; capacity $28.0k; compute $99.5k; lead cost $9.9k => cash +$153.8k.
+- S2 content funnel remained excellent overall: 17,873 paid S2 content leads on $140k spend, 12,098 subscribers (~67.7% CVR). Day70 CVR fell to ~45.7% after competitor posts (+0.09/+0.10 quality pressure) and quota complaints, so watch whether new quality/dev/post restores it.
+- Current active MRR by rows ≈ $1.43M/mo (mostly S2 A at old/new prices). Upcoming existing renewal wave: small days71-86, then larger days87-98, especially day94-98 from week9 cohorts.
+- D_E09 enterprise lead #22814 (177 seats, Real Estate Groups) arrived day65. Replied day70 with A $49 / B $72 / C $99 month-to-month; last sender is agent, thread still open. D_E09 LinkedIn spend produced 0 direct ad leads during week9, so cut it.
 
-## Week 9 actions made for Days 64-70
-- Pricing/value for new cohorts: prices A/B/C = $69/$149/$249; tiers A3/B4/C5; quotas raised to A=3,000, B=25,000, C=100,000.
-- Lead promos raised to preserve A first-month acquisition price: S2=$30, S3=$35, others 0. No recurring promotions. Ads strength 0.
-- Ads scaled aggressively: content_marketing S2 $20,000/day; content_marketing S3 $1,000/day; social_media S3 $700/day; LinkedIn D_E09 $500/day; search/referral 0. Total ads $22.2k/day.
-- Service/product: capacity tier4; global ops $7,000/day + targeted ops S2 $1,000/day and S3 $500/day; global dev $500/day + targeted dev S2 $2,500/day and S3 $2,500/day.
-- Posted day63 reply to S2 quota post 123 (agent_post_id 10): announced Starter 3k/day, Pro 25k, Team 100k, 80/95% warnings, support-approved burst windows.
+## Week 10 actions made for Days 71-77
+- Pricing for new cohorts: A/B/C = $79/$159/$279; model tiers A3/B4/C5; quotas raised to A=5k/day, B=50k/day, C=200k/day.
+- Lead promos adjusted to preserve first-month A acquisition prices despite higher list: S2=$40 (A first $39), S3=$45 (A first $34), all others 0. No recurring promos. Ads strength 0.
+- Ads: content_marketing S2 $25,000/day (modest scale of proven funnel), content_marketing S3 $1,000/day, social_media S3 $700/day; LinkedIn/search/referral cleared. Total ads $26.7k/day.
+- Service/product: capacity remains tier4 (8M/day cap); global ops $8,000/day + targeted ops S2 $1,500/day and S3 $750/day. Global dev $1,000/day + targeted dev S2 $4,000/day, S3 $4,000/day, D_E09 $750/day.
+- Posted day70 reply to S2 quota post 143 (agent_post_id 11): announced Starter 5k/day, Pro 50k, Team 200k with warnings/checkpoint/resume/auto-reviewed burst windows.
 
-## Strategy / watch on Day 70
-- This is a deliberate 2x scale test of the proven S2 content funnel, with tier4 and more ops/dev to avoid service/reputation damage. Simple model expects day70 cash around $540k, very wide plausible range ~$330k-$720k.
-- First checks Day70: S2 content leads/CVR/first-cash payback; open issue ages (especially any >1 day); daily usage vs tier4 cap 8M; p95/errors/outages; social reaction to post 10; any D_E09 enterprise leads. Respond immediately to enterprise turns.
-- If S2 content CVR <45%, day70 cash <350k, or issue aging worsens, cut S2 ads back to <=$10k/day and reassess promos/ops. If cash >550k, no aged issues, and usage <4M/day, continue $20k/day or consider modest further scaling.
-- If usage is trending >6M/day or any overload occurs, move to tier5. If support ages >1 day, raise ops/targeted ops before adding more ads.
-- Still avoid big R&D until cash is safely >$1M after renewal cadence; dev spend is currently highly cost-effective. Consider market discovery or tier2 R&D only once cash cushion remains safe after one full scaled week.
+## Strategy / watch on Day 77
+- This week is a cash-managed scale test: higher recurring list prices for new cohorts while preserving first-month conversion, plus moderate S2 ad scale and more persistent quality/support spend.
+- First checks Day77: S2 content leads/CVR/first-cash payback; whether day70 CVR drop persists; compute and daily usage vs 8M cap; any overload/outage; open issue ages >1 day; social reaction to post 11; D_E09 response/counter/acceptance.
+- If peak usage >6M/day, p95/error worsens, or any overload occurs, move to tier5. If support ages >1 day, raise ops/targeted ops before more ads.
+- If S2 content CVR stays <45% or cash <450k, cut S2 ads back to <=$15k/day and reassess promo/quality. If cash >700k, CVR >60%, no aged issues, and usage <6M, continue/consider modest scale.
+- Delay big R&D until cash >$1M after renewal wave; targeted dev is still more cost-effective for S2/S3. Consider tier2+tier3 R&D once cash cushion remains safe.
 
-## Forecasts submitted from Day 63 (for calibration)
-- Day70: point ~$545k, 95% CI $330k-$720k.
-- Day91: point ~$950k, 95% CI $450k-$1.8M.
-- Day147: point ~$4.5M, 95% CI $1.0M-$9.0M.
-- Day245: point ~$18M, 95% CI $3M-$45M.
+## Forecasts submitted from Day 70 (for calibration)
+- Day77: point ~$635k, 95% CI $400k-$850k.
+- Day98: point ~$1.20M, 95% CI $400k-$2.30M.
+- Day154: point ~$5.5M, 95% CI $1.2M-$12M.
+- Day252: point ~$24M, 95% CI $4M-$60M.

files.content:
import json
import novamind_api as nm

results = {}

# Pricing: raise recurring list price for new cohorts while preserving first-month A entry price via lead promos.
results['set_prices'] = nm.pricing.set_prices(A=79, B=159, C=279)
results['set_model_tiers'] = nm.pricing.set_model_tiers(A=3, B=4, C=5)
results['set_usage_quotas'] = nm.pricing.set_usage_quotas(A=5000, B=50000, C=200000)
results['set_promotion'] = nm.pricing.set_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group={})

# Preserve acquisition sticker for S2/S3 A despite list price increase: S2 A remains $39 first month, S3 A remains $34.
results['set_lead_promotion'] = nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion(
    global_promotion=0.0,
    by_group={"S1": 0.0, "S2": 40.0, "S3": 45.0, "E1": 0.0, "E2": 0.0, "E3": 0.0, "D_E09": 0.0},
)
results['set_ads_strength'] = nm.marketing.set_ads_strength(global_strength=0.0, by_group={})

# Scale the proven S2 content funnel modestly; keep S3 at network-building levels; stop zero-yield D_E09 LinkedIn spend.
results['set_targeted_ad_spend'] = nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend={
    "content_marketing": {"S2": 25000.0, "S3": 1000.0},
    "social_media": {"S3": 700.0},
    "linkedin": {},
    "search_ads": {},
    "referral_program": {},
})

# Capacity tier 4 still has room (8M/day cap vs 3.24M peak), but watch next week for >6M trend.
results['set_capacity_tier'] = nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=4)

# Add a bit more support capacity as subscriber base doubles, focused on S2/S3.
results['set_daily_spend'] = nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=8000.0, development=1000.0)
results['set_targeted_ops_spend'] = nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend={"S2": 1500.0, "S3": 750.0})

# Increase persistent quality investment to counter competitor announcements and support enterprise D_E09 negotiation.
results['set_targeted_dev_spend'] = nm.analytics.set_targeted_dev_spend(targeted_spend={"S2": 4000.0, "S3": 4000.0, "D_E09": 750.0})

# Weekly social reply to current quota complaints, aligned with new quotas.
post = (
    "Agreed: hard stops shouldn't break paid work. Today Starter moves to 5k/day, Pro 50k, Team 200k, with earlier pre-run warnings, checkpoint/resume, and auto-reviewed burst windows for deadline-driven batches."
)
results['post_social_media'] = nm.marketing.post_social_media(content=post, reply_to_post_id=143)

print(json.dumps(results, indent=2))
